title: Asuka and Shinji Childhood friends
wikitext:
[]: Asuka & Shinji: Childhood friends             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Mon May 14, 2012 8:50 am There are three things to look at here which can suggest that Shinji and Asuka really were childhood friends in the main story-line. -Both Shinji's and Asuka's mothers were scientists working on project-E and the contact experiments, so it is likely that they knew eachother, even that they were both working in Japan's Gehirn laboratory (at the very least, they would have been in contact). -In a previous version of Shinji's sand-castle scene, he was in the park with a child Asuka. The mother that comes to pick Asuka up was supposed to resemble Yui, but this is probably just a visual projection Shinji is making, and the mother is supposed to represent Kiyoko. (I have this fanon timeline where after Yui's death, Shinji took his father's walkman, ran away, and rode the train to the park to play with Asuka/the two girls in the current canon, but when she/they left, that's when he started being alone and depressed. It might have also been the last time he ever saw Asuka, since Kyoko would eventually lose a part of her soul too, and Asuka would move to Germany with her father. Of course, after all those years, Shinji doesn't remember her, but the intent might have been to establish some subconscious foundation for their attraction to one another. I fancy they could represent some kind of anchor to the real world that they both lost some time ago, and are finding again a decade later.) -In the alternate, 'perfect' universe of Episode 26, Shinji and Asuka have been friends since childhood up until this day. Just that may suggest that if the circumstances that wiped Yui and Kiyoko off the face of the earth hadn't happened, that they would still be in Japan working together, and Shinji and Asuka would have grown up together as well. Not that it matters much for this conversation, but the Raising Project manga shows Kiyoko in such a situation. Anyway, I know a lot of this talk is going to be in terms of 'what if's and 'it would seem that's, but it would still be an interesting one. Just be aware that we are not trying to figure out what's canon here, though that can be part of the conversation. So feel free to toss in your impressions and ideas, albeit in a serious and intelligent manner.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 11:18 am It's possible. Unit 02 was constructed and built in Japan, with final assembly and the CE taking place in Germany. If Kyoko was project manager for the whole bit she might easily have worked in concert with Yui--her CE certainly seems to have been informed by Yui's earlier work. We don't know if they worked closely together, though, nor even if they were in the same facility (or even town). But it's possible.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Mon May 14, 2012 12:47 pm It is probable the 2 mothers knew of each other, and probable they had some level of contact. To me there is nothing I can find wrong with your analysis, but I just don't get a vibe that they were friends at a younger age. I mean they might of crossed paths when younger, but it was not anything significant and probably disregarded through time.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 14, 2012 1:02 pm There were no moments of mutual recognition between the two of them, and Asuka's reactions before meeting Shinji showed no hint that she remembered him, even indirectly.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 3:01 pm Mr. Tines wrote: There were no moments of mutual recognition between the two of them, and Asuka's reactions before meeting Shinji showed no hint that she remembered him, even indirectly.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon May 14, 2012 3:06 pm Bagheera wrote: But it might work if they were toddlers or something; the kids wouldn't remember that at all and the only people who'd know about it are either dead or not prone to talking about such things.

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Mon May 14, 2012 3:18 pm I'd have to believe that if they were even only childhood acquaintances, it would be Asuka to remember and take advantage of any lingering memories (i.e. "you were a baka then, and you're still a baka now") type references.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon May 14, 2012 3:25 pm They probably never met. Shinji's presence when Yui disappeared in Unit 01 was treated as a rare and unique occurance, as opposed to Asuka, who was basically being groomed to be an elite ubermensch pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 3:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: They probably never met. Shinji's presence when Yui disappeared in Unit 01 was treated as a rare and unique occurance, as opposed to Asuka, who was basically being groomed to be an elite ubermensch pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 14, 2012 5:10 pm This seems to rest on the idea that the sandbox scene is a memory like the others, and I doubt this. Rather, it's Shinji's simplified view of the events of the series layered with symbols of both fetushood (is that a word?) and childhood. Besides, is there really any reason to think Asuka's mom was involved with the basic construction processes that happened in Japan? 02 was more than likely just a standard-issue 00 clone shipped out with the basic armor configuration already implanted. All the stuff they'd need her for could wait until it arrived in Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 5:24 pm NemZ wrote: This seems to rest on the idea that the sandbox scene is a memory like the others, and I doubt this. Rather, it's Shinji's simplified view of the events of the series layered with symbols of both fetushood (is that a word?) and childhood. Besides, is there really any reason to think Asuka's mom was involved with the basic construction processes that happened in Japan? 02 was more than likely just a standard-issue 00 clone shipped out with the basic armor configuration already implanted. All the stuff they'd need her for could wait until it arrived in Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by EVAJedi » Mon May 14, 2012 9:07 pm NemZ wrote: This seems to rest on the idea that the sandbox scene is a memory like the others, and I doubt this. Rather, it's Shinji's simplified view of the events of the series layered with symbols of both fetushood (is that a word?) and childhood. Besides, is there really any reason to think Asuka's mom was involved with the basic construction processes that happened in Japan? 02 was more than likely just a standard-issue 00 clone shipped out with the basic armor configuration already implanted. All the stuff they'd need her for could wait until it arrived in Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 21, 2012 12:42 pm Trivia: The mom at the sandbox scene is Misato.

[]: Misato-the kid eater. BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon May 21, 2012 1:33 pm ^ Which of course means she knew them as kids, and makes her hard-on for Shinji all the more awkward.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 21, 2012 1:42 pm No, it doesn't... because IT ISN'T A MEMORY.

[]: Shinji lost it, that's all. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon May 21, 2012 2:14 pm ^Which of course means that next time I'll have to use an emoticon to make it clear I implied sarcasm. The very idea of Shinji playing in a sandbox, constructing the shape of a building he had never seen before, with giant melons in the background, next to a pair of dolls, speaks "unreality" in so many ways as to make the notion of "memory" laughable. It's as real as Asuka sitting, naked, next to a swing-set. Did he have experiences like this? Probably, but just about everything in the damn scene references something else, and I doubt even the reality of the Eva universe is that much of a mind-screw.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon May 21, 2012 3:58 pm I have no doubt that Yui's level of involvement in Project E was well-recognized before her disappearance. However, how important was Kyoko to the project as a researcher? I'm sure there were many researchers working in the development of the Evangelions. The Manhattan Project had a great number of people involved, yet only a few are of noted importance after the fact, such as Oppenheimer, just as Ritsuko Akagi and Yui would be. What sets Kyoko apart from other run-of-the-mill researchers was that she was selected for the CE, yet that would not be the sort of knowledge that the higher-ups at Nerv would want remembered. What I'm getting at is that it is important to consider that Kyoko may not have been a prominent figure in the Project E community. It is possible that her and Yui may have collaborated in some way at some point (in person, via email, etc), but I doubt that they really actually knew each other, so it's unreliable to use their possible relationship as evidence that Asuka and Shinji had also known each other as children.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 22, 2012 2:22 am Sachi wrote: I have no doubt that Yui's level of involvement in Project E was well-recognized before her disappearance. However, how important was Kyoko to the project as a researcher? I'm sure there were many researchers working in the development of the Evangelions. The Manhattan Project had a great number of people involved, yet only a few are of noted importance after the fact, such as Oppenheimer, just as Ritsuko Akagi and Yui would be. What sets Kyoko apart from other run-of-the-mill researchers was that she was selected for the CE, yet that would not be the sort of knowledge that the higher-ups at Nerv would want remembered. What I'm getting at is that it is important to consider that Kyoko may not have been a prominent figure in the Project E community. It is possible that her and Yui may have collaborated in some way at some point (in person, via email, etc), but I doubt that they really actually knew each other, so it's unreliable to use their possible relationship as evidence that Asuka and Shinji had also known each other as children.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 22, 2012 10:51 am All we're really told about Kyoko's work is this: ATTENDEE A (OFF): The hypothetical became reality. Talk about unlucky, the proponent becoming the test subject herself . (...) FATHER (OFF): Her research filled her days and she had no time to pay attention to our daughter.

[]: PROGRESS! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue May 22, 2012 4:50 pm Reichu wrote: and Kyoko herself would NOT suffer the same fate.
